name: Monarch's Desanguination
components: V, S, M (a drop of the caster's own blood)
description: You tear the lifeblood from every creature in a 60-foot radius. Each non-construct, non-undead creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On failure, they take 12d8 necrotic damage and you gain temporary hit points equal to half the damage dealt. On success, they take half damage and you gain no temporary hit points. The ground is slick with blood, and the air fills with a coppery mist. This spell leaves the caster's veins aching, but the stolen essence fortifies them beyond mortal limits.

name: Sovereign's Aether Burst
components: V, S, M (a core of pure aether crystal)
description: You unleash a cataclysmic pulse of raw aether. All creatures in a 100-foot radius must make a Dexterity saving throw. On failure, they take 20d6 radiant damage and are blinded for 1 minute. On success, half damage and no blindness. Undead and fiends have disadvantage on the save. The blast leaves a scorched, glassy crater and the caster momentarily glows with internal light. Despite the god-tier power, the strain may ignite your own veins—roll Spell Safety.

name: Gravebind Singularity
components: V, S, M (a black hole sliver from a fallen star)
description: You tear open a micro-singularity at a point you can see. All creatures within a 40-foot radius must make a Strength saving throw or be pulled toward the center, taking 8d10 force damage and being restrained. Each turn the singularity persists, it pulls creatures 20 feet inward, dealing 4d10 force damage at the end of their turn if they are restrained. The gravitational distortion rips flesh and shatters bone. The caster must maintain unwavering will, or the singularity collapses prematurely.

name: Blood Sovereign's Dominion
components: V, S, M (the caster's blood drawn into a chalice)
description: You seize control of the blood within all creatures of your choice in a 60-foot cone. Each target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On failure, you gain telepathic dominance over them as per the Dominate Monster spell, but with unlimited range as long as you maintain concentration. Controlled creatures have veins visibly pulsing in sync with your heartbeat. You can issue commands as a bonus action. If a creature dies under your control, its blood erupts in a 10-foot burst dealing 6d6 necrotic damage to nearby enemies.

name: Cataclysmic Vein Rupture
components: V, S, M (a dried artery of a powerful vampire)
description: You force the blood within up to six creatures you can see to explosively rupture. Each target must make a Constitution saving throw. On failure, they take 15d10 necrotic damage and begin bleeding profusely, losing 2d10 hit points at the start of each of their turns until a healer stops the bleeding. On success, half damage and no ongoing bleed. The sheer violence of this magic paints the area in gore and may inflict aether burn on the caster if their blood purity wavers.

name: Ancestral Blood Rite
components: V, S, M (blood from at least ten ancient warriors, each worth 50+ gp)
description: You perform a forbidden rite using the blood of ancient warriors to call forth the spirits of Slaughter-Kings and Plague-Queens. Upon completion, you summon up to three Blood Wraiths (use Nightwalker stats, but deal necrotic damage and are immune to piercing). They obey your mental commands for 24 hours. Additionally, a Blood Shrine is created at the site, oozing aether that boosts your spell safety DC by 5 for all necromancy spells cast within 100 feet for the next year. The shrine corrupts the land, and the spirits whisper secrets of eternal damnation.

name: Null-Core Detonation
components: V, S, M (a shattered focus from a dead archmage)
description: You detonate a core of anti-magic that tears through all spells and aether. All magical effects within 80 feet are dispelled (as per Dispel Magic at 8th level). Each creature in the area that can cast spells must make a Charisma saving throw. On failure, they take 10d8 force damage and lose all spell slots for 1 minute. On success, half damage and lose one spell slot. A caster who fails also gains 3 levels of aether burn (automatic damage per spell level). The shockwave leaves a dead zone where magic sputters for 1d4 rounds.

name: Eternal Sanguination
components: V, S, M (a vial of pure aether and a drop of the target's blood)
description: You transmute the blood of one creature into volatile aether. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On failure, it takes 10d10 necrotic damage immediately and at the start of each of its turns for the duration, and its blood becomes a ticking bomb. If it takes fire or force damage while the spell is active, it explodes in a 20-foot radius, dealing 20d10 force damage to all creatures (including itself). The explosion ends the spell. On successful save, the target takes half damage and the blood remains normal. This magic is so unstable that even the caster may suffer backlash if the target dies during transmutation.

name: Aetherial Bloodstorm
components: V, S, M (blood from a sacrificed archmage)
description: You summon a storm of blood and raw aether in a colossal cylinder. The area becomes heavily obscured. All creatures in the storm must make a Constitution saving throw at the start of their turns. On failure, they take 6d10 necrotic damage and are blinded until the end of their next turn. On success, half damage. Additionally, any spellcaster in the storm must make a concentration check to cast spells (DC 15 + spell level), or the spell fails and they take aether burn equal to the level. The storm feeds on blood: if a creature dies within it, the storm strengthens, increasing the damage by 1d10 for the rest of its duration.

name: God-Heart Awakening
components: V, S, M (the caster's own heart's blood, drawn with a silver needle)
description: You forcibly awaken a portion of your Sovereign blood, temporarily raising your effective blood purity to 100%. For the duration, you automatically pass all Spell Safety checks, your spells deal maximum damage, and you can cast one additional 9th-level spell (if you have a slot). However, the strain is immense: at the end of the minute, you take 20d10 force damage that cannot be reduced, and your actual blood purity drops by 10% permanently (this can reduce you below the threshold to cast high-level spells until restored via rare rituals). Your veins glow with god-light, and those nearby sense your terrifying ascendance.

name: Phantom Blood Lance
components: V, S, M (a lance of iron dipped in the caster's blood)
description: You conjure a massive lance of congealed, crystalline blood that streaks toward a target. Make a ranged spell attack. On hit, the target takes 16d10 piercing damage plus 8d10 necrotic damage, and its blood begins to crystallize. The target must make a Constitution saving throw or have its speed reduced to 0 and be unable to take reactions until the end of its next turn. The lance then explodes, dealing 6d10 force damage to all creatures within 15 feet of the target. The shards remain as difficult terrain. The caster's own blood pressure spikes dangerously; if the attack misses, the lance shatters in the caster's hands, dealing 4d10 necrotic damage to the caster.

name: Soul-Cage Hemomancy
components: V, S, M (a diamond worth 5,000 gp, consumed, and the caster's own blood over the course of the hour)
description: You trap the soul of a recently deceased creature (died within 24 hours) within a blood-red crystal. The soul is aware and can communicate telepathically with you. While the crystal exists, you can use an action to command the soul to perform one task: answer a question truthfully (as per Commune, no limit on questions), possess a corpse nearby (as per Magic Jar), or unleash a burst of necrotic energy (10d10 in a 30-foot cone). The crystal has AC 15, 30 hit points, and requires a successful DC 20 Strength check to break. If smashed, the soul is released and cannot be recaptured. This spell is often used to torture and extract secrets from powerful enemies before their final death.

name: Soul-Sunder Cataclysm
components: V, S, M (a crystalline heart of a willing archon, worth 50,000 gp)
description: You shatter the boundary between life and death in a vast region. Every creature in the area must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 30). On a failure, their soul is ripped from their body and consumed by the aether, leaving a husk that rises as a thrall under your control. On a success, they take 20d10 necrotic damage and gain one level of exhaustion. The land itself becomes cursed; no resurrection magic works within the radius for 1d100 years.
safety_check: Constitution DC 20 (ignoring purity bonus due to soul-crushing backlash). On failure, caster takes 9d6 aether burn and their blood purity permanently drops by 1d10 percentage points.
purity_gate: Requires 70% purity or higher to attempt. If purity is below 95%, the caster must also succeed on a DC 25 Wisdom saving throw or be driven mad (indefinite madness).
special: If cast by a Sovereign (Awakened), the purity cost is negated, but the DM rolls on a world-shaking event table.

name: Graveheart Singularity
components: V, S, M (a black hole shard from a dead star, worth 100,000 gp)
description: You create a point of absolute gravitational collapse at the target location. All creatures and objects within a 500-foot radius must make a Strength saving throw (DC 30) or be pulled to the center, taking 30d10 bludgeoning damage and being restrained. The singularity persists; each round, the radius expands by 100 feet. Non-magical structures are crushed. At the end of the duration, the singularity implodes, dealing 50d10 force damage to everything within 1 mile and creating a permanent crater 2 miles wide.
safety_check: Constitution DC 18. On failure, caster takes 9d6 aether burn and is lifted 100 feet into the air by gravitational rebounding, taking 10d10 falling damage.
purity_gate: Requires 80% purity. The caster must also have a Strength score of at least 16 to withstand the pull.
special: If the caster loses concentration, the singularity detonates prematurely.

name: Endless Night's Ascendancy
components: V, S, M (a vial of shadow from the Plane of Darkness, absorbed into the caster's bloodstream)
description: You become a being of pure shadow and entropy. For the duration, you gain immunity to all damage except radiant, and you can pass through any solid object. Any creature that starts its turn within 30 feet of you must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 30) or take 10d10 necrotic damage and have its maximum hit points reduced by the same amount. You can also, as an action, extinguish all light sources within 1 mile (magical light requires a DC 25 dispel check). The sky above becomes pitch black, blocking the sun and stars. Crops wither, and the temperature drops to freezing.
safety_check: Constitution DC 17. On failure, you become permanently incorporeal (as a ghost) and lose your body. Only a Wish or Deific intervention can restore you.
purity_gate: Requires 85% purity. If the caster's purity is below 90%, they must also succeed on a Charisma saving throw (DC 20) or become an evil shadow creature under the DM's control.
special: While in this form, the caster cannot cast other spells.

name: Tear the Weave of Fate
components: V, S, M (a golden thread woven from the hair of a sphinx, worth 200,000 gp)
description: You reach into the fabric of causality and rip out a single event. Choose one event that occurred within the last 1,000 years (no larger than a continent-wide war or the death of a godlike being). That event is undone. All creatures that died in that event are resurrected (if their souls are still available). All consequences are reversed, but the timeline may splinter. The caster experiences immediate memory of both timelines and must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 25) or suffer indefinite madness.
safety_check: Constitution DC 20, disadvantage due to the strain of rewriting reality. On failure, the caster's body disintegrates into temporal dust. Their soul is lost in the timestream.
purity_gate: Requires 95% purity (only Sovereigns can attempt). Even then, the spell may provoke the wrath of time deities.
special: This spell cannot be undone. The DM determines side effects.

name: Plague of Unmaking
components: V, S, M (the corpse of a creature that died of a magical plague, preserved in aetherite jar)
description: You unleash a magical plague that spreads through the aether itself. Every creature within 10 miles must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 30) or contract the plague. Victims take 5d10 necrotic damage per day, cannot regain hit points, and each day the saving throw DC increases by 1. The plague is airborne and magical; it can cross planar boundaries. If a creature dies from the plague, it rises as a plague zombie that spreads the disease. There is no known cure except for a Greater Restoration cast at 9th level or the direct intervention of a deity.
safety_check: Constitution DC 19. On failure, the caster contracts the plague themselves (no save). They become the first patient zero and cannot be cured unless they succeed on a DC 30 Constitution check after 24 hours.
purity_gate: Requires 75% purity. If the caster's purity is below 90%, the plague may also affect allies without discrimination.
special: The plague cannot be dispelled; it is a supernatural curse.

name: Cataclysmic Geomancy
components: V, S, M (a tectonic plate shard from the Elemental Plane of Earth, held by the caster)
description: You cause massive geological upheaval. Choose a point on the ground: a 10-mile radius area becomes a volcano erupting magma (20d10 fire damage per round to everything inside, plus lava flows). Alternatively, you can cause an earthquake that splits the continent (creates a rift 1 mile wide and 10 miles long). Structures in a 100-mile radius collapse. Tsunamis are triggered if near water. The changes are permanent.
safety_check: Constitution DC 18. On failure, the caster is buried under collapsing earth (10d10 bludgeoning damage) and is restrained until freed.
purity_gate: Requires 80% purity. The caster must have a minimum Constitution of 18.
special: Cannot be cast indoors or underground without causing immediate collapse on caster.

name: Mind of the Dead God
components: V, S, M (the skull of a dead god, which crumbles to dust after casting)
description: You channel the consciousness of a deceased deity. For the duration, you can read the thoughts of any creature within 1 mile, and you can issue a single command that all creatures within range must obey (no save, unless they are immune to mind-affecting effects). If you choose, you can also absorb the god's memories—this gives you advantage on all Intelligence checks but also risks insanity (Wisdom save DC 25 or suffer permanent madness). After the spell ends, you are stunned for 1 round and take 10d10 psychic damage.
safety_check: Constitution DC 20. On failure, the god's personality overrides yours; you become an NPC under the DM's control (possibly a new villain).
purity_gate: Requires 90% purity. Even then, the caster's alignment shifts permanently to the god's alignment (usually evil).
special: Only one such skull exists in the world per god. Using this spell may attract the attention of living gods.

name: Soulforged Desolation
components: V, S, M (a soul jar containing 1,000 captured souls, which are released and consumed)
description: You open a rift to the Negative Energy Plane, allowing a tide of anti-life to flood the area. All non-undead creatures in the radius must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 30). On a failure, they take 40d10 necrotic damage and are reduced to 0 hit points. On a success, they take half damage and their maximum hit points are reduced by the damage taken. The area becomes a dead magic zone for 1d100 years; no magic functions there (including resurrection). The ground becomes lifeless and barren.
safety_check: Constitution DC 20. On failure, the caster's soul is sucked into the Negative Energy Plane; their body becomes a wraith under DM control.
purity_gate: Requires 95% purity OR the caster must be undead (lich/vampire). The spell consumes 1,000 souls from the jar.
special: Casting this spell is considered an atrocity; good-aligned deities may smite the caster.

name: Word of Unmaking
components: V (a guttural utterance that can be heard for 10 miles)
description: You speak a syllable that negates magic itself. All spells and magical effects of 8th level or lower in the cone are dispelled automatically. Creatures concentrating on spells must make a Constitution save (DC 30) or lose the spell. Additionally, any creature in the cone that is magically created (like summoned creatures, golems, etc.) must make a Charisma saving throw (DC 30) or be permanently destroyed. The sound itself deals 10d10 thunder damage to all creatures in the cone (no save). After casting, you cannot speak for 1 hour (magically silenced).
safety_check: Constitution DC 18. On failure, you are permanently deafened and take 10d10 psychic damage from the backlash of the word.
purity_gate: Requires 85% purity. The caster must know at least one language of primordial power (Draconic, Celestial, Infernal, etc.).
special: This word can also harm artifacts; roll a d20. On a 1, an artifact in range is destroyed.

name: Raynok's Final Storm
components: V, S, M (a lightning-rod from a storm giant's castle, charged by a god's wrath)
description: You call down a storm that covers a 10-mile radius. Within that area, the skies are pitch black, and every round, a lightning bolt strikes a random creature (10d10 lightning damage, Dex save DC 30 for half). Additionally, tornadoes form (5d10 bludgeoning damage each round to anything in their path). The storm also causes torrential rain that floods low-lying areas within 10 miles. The storm cannot be dispelled by any means less than a Wish or divine intervention. After the spell ends, the area is devastated.
safety_check: Constitution DC 17. On failure, the caster is struck by a bolt of lightning (20d10 lightning damage, no save) and the storm goes rogue (DM controls it).
purity_gate: Requires 80% purity. The caster must have a natural connection to storms (e.g., Storm Sorcery origin, or control weather spell known).
special: If the caster loses concentration, the storm dissipates over 1d4 hours, but the damage is done.

name: Pact of the Hollow Crown
components: V, S, M (a crown forged from the iron of a thousand hanged kings, worth 500,000 gp)
description: You create a permanent enchantment on a ruler or monarch (willing or unconscious) that binds their soul to a crown. The crown becomes an artifact that controls the ruler's mind; the ruler loses all free will and becomes a puppet. Additionally, all creatures within 1 mile that are loyal to the ruler (officials, guards, etc.) must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 30) or become enslaved to the crown as well. The crown can be used to issue commands telepathically across any distance. Destroying the crown kills the ruler.
safety_check: Constitution DC 20. On failure, the caster's soul is trapped inside the crown instead; they become the puppet.
purity_gate: Requires 90% purity. The caster must also sacrifice a humanoid of noble birth (their choice) or the spell fails and backfires.
special: This spell is considered a high crime against the natural order. Paladins and clerics may sense the disturbance.

name: Oblivion's Embrace
components: V, S, M (a shard from a destroyed plane, which disintegrates after casting)
description: You channel raw oblivion, the stuff of non-existence. Every creature in the cone must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 30). On a failure, they are erased from existence—no body, no soul, no afterlife. They cannot be resurrected by any means short of a divine act of creation. On a success, they take 30d10 force damage and are permanently blinded and deafened. The caster also takes 10d10 force damage as backlash. The ground in the cone becomes a grey, lifeless zone where nothing grows for 1,000 years.
safety_check: Constitution DC 20. On failure, the caster is also erased from existence. No save.
purity_gate: Requires 95% purity (only Sovereigns). If the caster is not fully awakened, they automatically fail the safety check.
special: This spell cannot be counterspelled. It does not generate aether burn; it consumes the caster's own essence instead.
